user.title: Dynamic Home
user.description: Which module do you want to add?

vmc.title: Dynamic Ventilation (VMC)
vmc.description: Select your VMC entities. Optional fields enable free-cooling (temperatures) and the outdoor-AQI cap.

data.name: Name
data.hum_in: Indoor humidity (optional, dew point / dry mode)

shutter.title: Dynamic Shutter
shutter.description: Select the shutter and its orientation. Climate and sensors are optional (enable solar shield, free-cooling and weather protection).

data.name: Name
data.facade_span_deg: Facade acceptance angle (° span, e.g. 90 = narrow, 180 = wide)

climate.title: Dynamic Climate (Zone)
climate.description: Climate zone. Publishes solar-gain (heat) / solar-shield (cool) intents to the configured shutters.

data.name: Name
data.dc_climate: Thermostat to drive (optional; advisory if empty)
data.ds_target: Shutter target on the bus (e.g. ds)

weather.title: Dynamic Weather
weather.description: Resilient multi-source forecast/alert provider. Lists weather sources in priority order with fallback; the user's own sensors can be plugged in too.

zones.title: Dynamic Home · Zones
zones.description: Create the zone → group → house hierarchy (one entry). Assign modules to zones and zones to groups from this entry's options.

energy.title: Dynamic Home · Energy
energy.description: House energy brain (one entry). Publishes context — import headroom under the contracted power, tariff state, scarcity — that other modules read to modulate their aggressiveness; it never commands. Plug in your own entities; it works with any subset (PV/battery are optional).

changeover.title: Community changeover
changeover.description: For a 2-pipe community radiant system the building sends hot OR cold water to everyone by season. Point this at the supply-water temperature sensor; community zones then heat or cool in the direction the building currently allows. Use the «Changeover (water)» selector to force a direction (auto reads the sensor).

data_description.changeover_sensor: A temperature sensor on the manifold / supply pipe. Leave empty to drive the changeover only manually.
data_description.heat_above_c: In auto, supply water at or above this means the building is heating.
data_description.cool_below_c: In auto, supply water at or below this means the building is cooling. In between is treated as idle (shoulder season).
data_description.hysteresis_c: Anti-flap band: once heating/cooling, the water must move this far past the threshold before the direction changes.

presence.title: Presence
presence.description: Fuse your own presence sources per zone (PIR, mmWave, door) into Occupied/Empty, and the house into Home/Away/Sleeping. Works with any subset of sources.

presence_house.title: Presence · house
presence_house.description: Phones (device_tracker / person) decide Home/Away at house level. Auto-drive sets the house mode (Home/Away/Sleeping) from presence — it never overrides a manual Boost/Eco. The sleep window bounds Sleeping detection.

presence_zone_detail.title: Presence · {zone}
presence_zone_detail.description: Pick this zone's sources. mmWave holds presence through stillness (sitting still won't read Empty); a door contact lets the house go Away when the last zone empties.

cat_iaq.title: Air-quality thresholds
cat_iaq.description: At what CO2/PM the fan steps up. Lower the thresholds for stricter air; the hysteresis prevents flapping.

data_description.co2_v2: CO₂ level (ppm) that raises the VMC to V2. Thresholds decide when to step up.
data_description.co2_v3: CO₂ level (ppm) that raises the VMC to V3.
data_description.pm_v2: PM2.5 level (µg/m³) that raises the VMC to V2.
data_description.pm_v3: PM2.5 level (µg/m³) that raises the VMC to V3.
data_description.co2_hys: CO₂ drop margin before stepping down. Prevents sawtooth; if it oscillates, raise it a bit.
data_description.pm_hys: PM2.5 drop margin before stepping down (anti sawtooth).

cat_smoothing.title: Sensor smoothing
cat_smoothing.description: Smooths CO2/PM with an EMA so it doesn't react to noise. Higher alpha = more reactive.

data_description.co2_ema_enabled: Smooth CO₂ with an EMA before deciding (less jitter).
data_description.pm_ema_enabled: Smooth PM2.5 with an EMA before deciding.
data_description.co2_ema_alpha: CO₂ EMA factor (higher = more reactive, less smoothing).

cat_freecool.title: Free-cooling
cat_freecool.description: Pulls in cool outside air when it's warmer indoors. Configure the changeover so it doesn't vent heat in winter.

data_description.freecool_t_ext_min: Do not free-cool below this outdoor temp (too cold).
data_description.freecool_delta_on: Indoor−outdoor ΔT that starts free-cooling. (↓ = starts with less difference)
data_description.freecool_delta_off: ΔT that stops free-cooling (hysteresis). (↓ = holds with less difference)

cat_hostile.title: Hostile outdoor air
cat_hostile.description: Throttles ventilation when the outdoor air is hostile (extreme cold).

data_description.hostile_t1: Outdoor AQI that caps the VMC at V2 (outside starting to be bad). (↓ = reacts sooner to bad air)
data_description.hostile_t2: Outdoor AQI that caps the VMC at V1. (↓ = reacts sooner to bad air)
data_description.hostile_t3: Outdoor AQI that turns the VMC off (don't pull very bad air). (↓ = reacts sooner to bad air)

cat_dry.title: Anti-condensation
cat_dry.description: Ventilates to dry only when the outside air is genuinely drier (dew point + margin), not by raw humidity.

data_description.dry_v2_delta: Dew-point advantage (dp_indoor − dp_outdoor, °C) to dry at V2. (↓ = dries with less dew advantage)
data_description.dry_v3_delta: Dew-point advantage to dry at V3. (↓ = dries with less dew advantage)
data_description.dew_spread_min: Indoor temp within this of the dew point flags condensation risk. (↑ = cuts sooner, more conservative)
data_description.dry_margin: Minimum dew-point advantage (dp_indoor − dp_outdoor, °C) before drying ventilation engages. (↓ = ventilates to dry more readily)
data_description.dry_hys: Hysteresis band: drying disengages once the advantage falls below margin − hysteresis.

cat_failsafe.title: Failsafe & startup
cat_failsafe.description: Protects against dead sensors and bounces: staleness, startup grace, anti-trip. Touch with care.

data_description.co2_sanity_floor: CO₂ readings below this (ppm) are treated as a sensor fault and routed to the failsafe, since an occupied room can't be below the ~410 ppm atmospheric baseline. PM2.5 is not floored.
data_description.stale_threshold_s: A vital sensor older than this (s) counts as failed.
data_description.startup_grace_s: Grace period after start before the failsafe can trip (s).
data_description.trip_window_s: Window (s) over which repeated trips are counted. (↑ = counts faults over a longer window)
data_description.trip_limit: Trips within the window that arm a lockout. (↓ = locks out after fewer faults)
data_description.lockout_s: How long the failsafe lockout lasts (s). (↑ = longer lockout)

cat_shower.title: Shower boost
cat_shower.description: Boost on a RISE of the bathroom's own RH over its baseline (not the delta with outdoors), with a hold. Outdoor humidity only decides whether expelling helps.

data_description.shower_rh_delta_on: Bathroom RH rise over its baseline (%) that triggers the boost. A shower spikes it; a steady level doesn't. If it detects false showers, raise this.
data_description.shower_rh_delta_off: Rise below which the boost ends (hysteresis). If it cuts too soon, lower this.
data_description.shower_effective_margin: How much drier (%RH) the outside must be for expelling to help. 0 = expel whenever the bathroom is above outdoors. (↑ = stricter)
data_description.shower_baseline_alpha: How fast the baseline tracks the real humidity (frozen during a shower). (↑ = adapts sooner, detects short showers worse)
data_description.shower_hold_s: Minimum hold time for the shower boost (seconds). If it cuts too soon, raise it.
data_description.shower_level: Target speed while a shower is detected (V2/V3). (V3 ventilates harder than V2)

cat_adaptive_iaq.title: Adaptive thresholds
cat_adaptive_iaq.description: How many samples before trusting the learned thresholds.

data_description.adaptive_min_samples: Readings needed before adaptive IAQ thresholds are used.

cat_positions.title: Shutter positions
cat_positions.description: The target positions per situation (rain, summer shield, night, alert...). They set what % the shutter goes to in each branch.

data_description.rain_close_pct: Shutter position when it rains (protection). (↓ = more closed, more protection)
data_description.freecool_max_open_pct: Max opening allowed during free-cooling. (↑ = allows opening more)
data_description.summer_min_open_pct: Minimum opening kept in summer. (↑ = keeps it more open in summer)
data_description.winter_night_pct: Position on winter nights (close to insulate). (↓ = closes more to insulate)
data_description.weather_max_open_pct: Max opening under a weather alert.
data_description.sdhb_solar_shield_max_open_pct: Max opening when DC asks for solar shielding (cooling). (↓ = shades more on shield)

cat_thermal.title: Thermal deltas
cat_thermal.description: Temperature deltas that trigger free-cooling / the solar shield (how much warmer outside than inside).

data_description.freecool_delta: Indoor−outdoor ΔT that justifies opening for free-cooling. (↓ = opens to purge with less difference)
data_description.hot_delta: ΔT above which the room counts as hot (shading logic). (↓ = counts as hot sooner)

cat_shield.title: Thermal shield
cat_shield.description: Opt-in (turn on the 'Thermal shield' switch). Thermal priority by season. COOLING with hotter outside: the shutter never opens past 'Max open while cooling' (0 = fully shut), with or without sun — the geometric shading can still close further. HEATING with sun: captures gain up to 'Max open while heating' (100 = full gain); by day with no sun it closes to insulate only when it's colder outside (Cold ΔT). Off = legacy.

data_description.heat_shield_pct: Max opening while cooling and it's hotter outside (preserve the cool). 0 = fully shut; raise it to let some daylight in. (↓ = blocks more heat)
data_description.heat_max_open_pct: Max opening while heating with direct sun (solar gain). 100 = full gain; lower it to limit glare or over-gain. (↓ = captures less sun)
data_description.cold_delta: In heating, ΔT below which the outside counts as cold: by day with no direct sun, the shutter closes to insulate only when it's this much colder outside (else it stays open for light). (↓ = insulates sooner)

cat_wind.title: Wind protection
cat_wind.description: Wind cap with hysteresis to protect the slats. Works with Weather alerts.

data_description.wind_limit_kmh: Wind speed that forces protective closing. (↓ = closes at less wind)
data_description.wind_cap_span_kmh: Range above the limit over which opening is progressively capped. (↑ = ramps the cap more gradually)
data_description.wind_cap_hyst_kmh: Hysteresis to release the wind cap. (↑ = releases the cap later)

cat_slew.title: Progressive movement
cat_slew.description: Limits how much the shutter position can change each cycle (smoother, fewer motor starts).

data_description.slew_enabled: Move the shutter in steps instead of jumping (smoother, fewer motor starts).
data_description.slew_step_pct: Max % the position changes per step. (↓ = smoother, slower moves)

cat_geometry.title: Window geometry
cat_geometry.description: Window/overhang measurements for the geometric shading model (opt-in). Only matters with 'Geometric shading' on.

data_description.window_height_cm: Window height for the solar-penetration geometry. (your window's real measurement)
data_description.overhang_cm: Overhang/eave depth above the window. (your overhang's real measurement)
data_description.overhang_offset_cm: Vertical gap between the overhang/eave and the top of the window; the eave only shades the glass below this gap. Covered terraces: the roof edge sits well above, so use a larger value. (↑ = the eave shades less)
data_description.sill_height_cm: Window sill height above the floor (geometric shading). (your sill's real measurement)
data_description.room_depth_m: Floor depth of the room; sun penetration is capped here. (your room's real depth)
data_description.target_penetration_m: Allowed sunlit depth into the room before the geometric shading closes the shutter.
data_description.shade_step_pct: Quantization step of the geometric shutter position. (↑ = coarser position steps)

cat_setpoints.title: Base setpoints
cat_setpoints.description: The starting point of the target; outdoor/VMC/facade biases, the lead and the brake all stack on top. Set these to your real comfort and let the rest modulate.

data_description.base_heat_day: Daytime starting point when heating. Feeds the Active Base and, in cascade, the target. Set it to your real comfort (e.g. 22.0–22.8 °C) and let the biases do the rest. (↑ = warmer)
data_description.base_cool_day: Daytime starting point when cooling. Start conservative (26–27 °C) to avoid aggressive cooling and oscillation. (↓ = cooler)
data_description.delta_night: How much the base eases at night (sun below −3°): lowers the setpoint when heating, raises it when cooling. If you get too warm at night, raise it gradually (0.1 steps).
data_description.vac_base_heat_day: Heating base while Vacation mode is on (energy-saving reduced setpoint).
data_description.vac_base_cool_day: Cooling base while Vacation mode is on (energy-saving raised setpoint). (↑ = more saving on vacation)

cat_limits.title: Setpoint limits
cat_limits.description: Safety net: how far the target may move and how often it is applied. If the target ever drifts out of a sane range, bound it here.

data_description.target_min_heat: Lowest allowed setpoint when heating (normal mode). The final target is never clamped below this.
data_description.target_max_heat: Highest allowed setpoint when heating (normal mode). The final target is never clamped above this.
data_description.target_min_cool: Lowest allowed setpoint when cooling (normal mode). (bounds the cooling target)
data_description.target_max_cool: Highest allowed setpoint when cooling (normal mode). (bounds the cooling target)
data_description.step: Quantization step for the final target (rounding), e.g. 0.1 °C. (↑ = coarser rounding, fewer micro-moves)
data_description.max_mods_heat: Hard cap on the combined push of all biases/brakes in heat, before the limited target. If it falls short, raise it in small steps; if it gets aggressive, lower it or review the biases first.
data_description.max_mods_cool: Hard cap on the combined push of all biases/brakes in cool (same idea as the heat cap). (↑ = lets biases move the target more)
data_description.apply_min_delta: Minimum change vs the last setpoint before pushing a new one to the thermostat (anti-jitter). 0 applies always; with a small step (0.1) try 0.2.
data_description.vac_target_min_heat: Lowest heating setpoint while on vacation. (bounds the vacation target)
data_description.vac_target_max_heat: Highest heating setpoint while on vacation. (bounds the vacation target)
data_description.vac_target_min_cool: Lowest cooling setpoint while on vacation. (bounds the vacation target)
data_description.vac_target_max_cool: Highest cooling setpoint while on vacation. (bounds the vacation target)

cat_exterior.title: Outdoor bias
cat_exterior.description: How much the outdoor temperature pushes the target. Raise the biases to react more to cold/heat outside; lower insulation_factor if the home is well insulated.

data_description.ext_cold_threshold: Outdoor temp at/below which a heating bias kicks in (cold compensation).
data_description.ext_hot_threshold: Outdoor temp at/above which a cooling bias kicks in (hot compensation). (↓ = the heat bias kicks in sooner)
data_description.bias_ext_heat_strong: Heating bias when it is clearly cold (below the threshold).
data_description.bias_ext_heat_mild: Heating bias in the mild band just above the cold threshold. (↑ = compensates the outdoors more)
data_description.bias_ext_cool_strong: Cooling bias when it is clearly hot. (↑ = compensates the outdoors more)
data_description.bias_ext_cool_mild: Cooling bias in the mild band near the hot threshold. (↑ = compensates the outdoors more)
data_description.insulation_factor: 0–1 multiplier on the outdoor bias: lower it for a well-insulated home.

cat_bus.title: Bus self-bias
cat_bus.description: How much the solar bus self-biases this zone (gain/shield intents). Works together with Facade bias and the shutter module.

data_description.sdhb_bias_solar_gain_heat: Setpoint relief when DC receives a solar-gain intent while heating (free sun warms the room). (↑ = leans more on free sun)
data_description.sdhb_bias_solar_shield_cool: Setpoint raise when DC receives a solar-shield intent while cooling.

cat_vmc.title: VMC bias
cat_vmc.description: Compensates the target for the VMC speed (more ventilation = more loss), per speed and mode.

data_description.vmc_bias_heat_1: Heating compensation when the VMC runs at V1 (ventilation loads the room). (↑ = compensates ventilation more)
data_description.vmc_bias_heat_2: Heating compensation when the VMC runs at V2. (↑ = compensates ventilation more)
data_description.vmc_bias_heat_3: Heating compensation when the VMC runs at V3. (↑ = compensates ventilation more)
data_description.vmc_bias_cool_1: Cooling compensation when the VMC runs at V1. (↑ = compensates ventilation more)
data_description.vmc_bias_cool_2: Cooling compensation when the VMC runs at V2. (↑ = compensates ventilation more)
data_description.vmc_bias_cool_3: Cooling compensation when the VMC runs at V3. (↑ = compensates ventilation more)

cat_trend.title: Trend & lead
cat_trend.description: The anticipation (lead): how many hours ahead the target reaches. Raise the lead to pre-heat/pre-cool earlier — but if you raise it, watch the Brake so you don't overshoot. lead_min/max cap the result.

data_description.trend_lead_h: Fallback anticipation (hours) when temperatures are unavailable. (↑ = anticipates earlier without the model)
data_description.trend_max_shift: Max °C the trend can shift the target per cycle (anti-spike). (↑ = the trend moves the target more)
data_description.lead_per_degree_h: Extra anticipation per °C of indoor/outdoor gap (inertia). (↑ = more anticipation with inertia)
data_description.lead_wind_h_per_kmh: Extra anticipation per km/h of wind (higher losses).
data_description.lead_min_h: Lower clamp on the computed lead (hours). (bounds the computed lead)
data_description.lead_max_h: Upper clamp on the computed lead (hours). (bounds the computed lead)
data_description.trend_deadband_cph: Trend below this (°C/h) is treated as zero (noise).

cat_brake.title: Trend brake
cat_brake.description: Brakes the target when the trend already pushes toward setpoint, to avoid overshooting. The natural pair of the lead: more anticipation -> more brake.

data_description.brake_thresholds_1: Trend speed (°C/h) for brake step 1 (only when the trend already helps the mode). (↓ = brakes sooner)
data_description.brake_thresholds_2: Trend speed (°C/h) for brake step 2. (↓ = brakes sooner)
data_description.brake_thresholds_3: Trend speed (°C/h) for brake step 3. (↓ = brakes sooner)
data_description.brake_biases_1: °C the brake subtracts at step 1 to avoid overshoot. (↑ = brakes harder)
data_description.brake_biases_2: °C the brake subtracts at step 2. (↑ = brakes harder)
data_description.brake_biases_3: °C the brake subtracts at step 3. (↑ = brakes harder)

cat_forecast.title: Forecast
cat_forecast.description: Uses the weather forecast to nudge the target. Gain sets how much it weighs; cap limits it.

data_description.forecast_gain: How strongly the forecast eases the setpoint (°C per °C of expected help). Brake-only. (↑ = forecast relaxes more)
data_description.forecast_cap: Max °C the forecast bias can apply. (↑ = allows more forecast relief)
data_description.forecast_window_h: Look-ahead window (hours) scanned for the helping extreme. (↑ = looks further ahead)

cat_adaptive_lead.title: Adaptive lead
cat_adaptive_lead.description: The model that LEARNS your thermal inertia (rate/lag) and sets the lead by itself. Advanced: these knobs tune the learning — you rarely need to touch them.

data_description.adapt_alpha: EMA smoothing of the learned rate/overshoot/lag. (↑ = learns faster but noisier)
data_description.adapt_gain_lr: Learning step toward the lead target each cycle. (↑ = converges to the target lead faster)
data_description.adapt_overshoot_target: Tolerated overshoot (°C) before the lead is corrected.
data_description.adapt_rate_floor_cph: Floor on the learned heating/cooling rate (avoids divide-by-zero). (advanced; safety floor)
data_description.adapt_lag_k: Weight of the learned thermal lag in the lead. (↑ = lag weighs more in the lead)
data_description.adapt_on_rate_min_dt_h: Min ON duration (h) to trust a rate sample. (↑ = requires cleaner samples)
data_description.adapt_on_rate_min_dt: Min temperature change (°C) to trust a rate sample. (↑ = requires cleaner samples)
data_description.adapt_off_window_h: Window (h) watched after OFF to learn overshoot and lag. (↑ = observes longer after off)
data_description.lead_adaptive_min_h: Lower clamp on the learned lead (hours). (bounds the learned lead)
data_description.lead_adaptive_max_h: Upper clamp on the learned lead (hours). (bounds the learned lead)

cat_condensation.title: Condensation
cat_condensation.description: Margin to the dew point before cutting cooling (anti-condensation).

data_description.dew_spread_min: Condensation risk when indoor temp is within this margin of the dew point (radiant-cooling safety). (↑ = cuts sooner, more conservative)

cat_facade.title: Facade bias
cat_facade.description: Push from sun entering each facade. Works with Bus self-bias and the shutters.

data_description.facade_gain_heat: Max setpoint relief from sunlit open facades while heating (free solar gain). (↑ = leans more on facade sun)
data_description.facade_gain_cool: Max setpoint relief from sunlit facades while cooling. (↑ = leans more on facade sun)

data_description.preset: Loads a ready-made set of values for a typical installation. You can fine-tune any field afterwards.

cat_filter.title: Filter life
cat_filter.description: Filter life hours before the replacement notice.

data_description.filter_life_hours: Total hours a filter lasts before replacement; the filter-life % sensor counts down from this value.

cat_anticipatory.title: Anticipatory boost
cat_anticipatory.description: Gets ahead of a fast CO2/PM rise by its slope, before it crosses the threshold.

data_description.anticip_co2_rate_on: CO₂ rise rate (ppm/h) that pre-boosts ventilation before the absolute level is reached.
data_description.anticip_co2_rate_off: Rate below which the anticipatory boost releases (hysteresis).
data_description.anticip_pm_rate_on: PM2.5 rise rate (µg/m³/h) that pre-boosts ventilation.
data_description.anticip_pm_rate_off: PM2.5 rate below which the anticipatory boost releases.
data_description.anticip_hold_s: Minimum hold so a brief spike doesn't cause oscillation (seconds). (↑ = holds the boost longer)
data_description.anticip_level: Target speed while a steep rise is detected (V1/V2/V3).
data_description.anticip_ema_alpha: Smoothing of the slope itself (higher = more reactive).

cat_quiet.title: Quiet hours
cat_quiet.description: Speed cap during the night window — but critical CO2/PM override it (health wins over silence).

data_description.quiet_critical_co2: During quiet hours the speed is capped, but a CO₂ at or above this lifts the cap (health > silence). (↓ = lifts the cap sooner)
data_description.quiet_critical_pm: PM2.5 at or above this lifts the quiet-hours cap. (↓ = lifts the cap sooner)

data_description.hrv_bypass_eff_max: Efficiency (0..1) at or below which, with a real ΔT, the exchanger is considered in bypass / not recovering.
data_description.hrv_bypass_dt_min: Minimum extract−intake ΔT (°C) needed before judging recovery vs bypass. (advanced)

cat_demand.title: Real demand signal
cat_demand.description: Threshold to read the real demand (valve relay/power) instead of inferring it.

data_description.valve_power_min: When the valve source is numeric (power, W), a reading above this counts the valve/relay as active. (↑ = needs more power to count as on)

cat_mold.title: Mold risk
cat_mold.description: Hours-of-high-humidity index that arms drying/dehumidifier. On/off hysteresis + decay.

data_description.mold_rh_threshold: Relative humidity at/above which mold-risk hours accrue. (↓ = more sensitive to mold)
data_description.mold_on_h: Accumulated hours that arm the alert and drying. (↓ = warns sooner)
data_description.mold_off_h: Accumulated hours below which it disarms (hysteresis).
data_description.mold_decay_h: Exponential decay time constant while RH is below the threshold.
data_description.mold_cap_h: Maximum value the index can reach. (index ceiling)

cat_window.title: Open-window detection
cat_window.description: Detects an open window from a sharp temperature drop. Confirm/recovery timers + a lockout cap.

data_description.window_drop_cph: Indoor-temperature change against the demand (°C/h) that flags a likely open window (only when no window sensor is configured). (↓ = detects the window on gentler drops)
data_description.window_confirm_min: Minutes the anomaly must persist before the zone is switched off.
data_description.window_release_min: Minutes of stable temperature before normal control resumes.
data_description.window_max_lockout_min: Safety timeout that resumes control even if the temperature has not stabilised. (↑ = waits longer before resuming)

cat_adjacent.title: Adjacent space
cat_adjacent.description: Advisories from an adjacent space (a door open to a very different zone).

data_description.adj_open_dt: In heat, advise opening the door when the adjacent space is at least this much warmer than indoors (free solar gain).
data_description.adj_alarm_dt: In cool, warn when the door is open and the adjacent space is at least this much warmer than indoors.

data_description.expose_mirrors: Create a stable mirror sensor for each configured input (temperature, humidity, CO₂, …) so dashboards keep working when you replace a physical sensor — only the entry needs reconfiguring. Off by default.

cat_dawn.title: Gradual sunrise
cat_dawn.description: Gentle sunrise ramp: step, interval, target and the trigger elevation.

data_description.dawn_step_pct: How much the shutter opens at each step.
data_description.dawn_target_pct: Opening the ramp climbs to. (↑ = ends more open)
data_description.dawn_trigger_elevation: Sun elevation (degrees) that starts the ramp at dawn. (↑ = starts with the sun higher)

cat_night.title: Night insulation
cat_night.description: Night-insulation positions (close to insulate / open to purge the thermal mass).

data_description.night_iso_close_pct: Closed position at night to insulate (heat) or protect the thermal mass (cool, warm night). (↓ = closes more to insulate)
data_description.night_iso_open_pct: Open position at night to purge the thermal mass when the outside is cooler (cool).

cat_alert.title: Weather alerts
cat_alert.description: Protection position per alert (generic/hail/wind) + how long to hold it. A safety cap above the solar logic.

data_description.alert_pct: Protection position for the generic weather alert. (↓ = more closed, more protection)
data_description.alert_hail_pct: Protection position for a hail/storm alert (fully closed protects best). (↓ = more closed, more protection)
data_description.alert_wind_pct: Protection position for a wind alert (a mid position can protect the slats). (↓ = more closed, more protection)
data_description.alert_hold_min: Minutes the protection is held after the alert clears. (↑ = protects longer after the alert)

data_description.hood_pm_v1: Indoor PM2.5 at/above which the hood runs at V1. (↓ = the hood steps up sooner)
data_description.hood_pm_v2: Indoor PM2.5 at/above which the hood runs at V2. (↓ = the hood steps up sooner)
data_description.hood_pm_v3: Indoor PM2.5 at/above which the hood runs at V3. (↓ = the hood steps up sooner)
data_description.hood_hys: Margin the PM must fall below before the hood steps down a level.

cat_wx.title: Sources & alerts
cat_wx.description: Weather sources and alerts (wind/rain/etc.) that feed the protections.

data_description.stale_after_h: A source whose data is older than this counts as down (fall back to the next).
data_description.alert_wind_kmh: Wind at/above this raises the weather alert. (↓ = alerts sooner)
data_description.alert_precip_mm: Precipitation at/above this raises the weather alert. (↓ = alerts sooner)

cat_tariff.title: Tariff thresholds
cat_tariff.description: Tariff bands (cheap/normal/peak) the energy module publishes for the climate zones to use.

data_description.cheap_below: At or below this price the tariff state is «cheap». With no price sensor a fixed tariff is used.
data_description.peak_above: At or above this price the tariff state is «peak».

cat_cycle.title: Short-cycle protection
cat_cycle.description: Protects the compressor: minimum ON/OFF and max starts/hour. Note: per-zone priority does NOT live here — it lives in Electrical peak. You can let 'Adaptive anti-cycle' size these from learning.

data_description.anticycle_min_on_s: Minimum time the shared compressor stays on before a stop is registered (protects against short cycling). Safety stops override it.
data_description.anticycle_min_off_s: Minimum time the compressor stays off before it may restart.
data_description.anticycle_max_starts_per_h: Maximum compressor starts allowed per rolling hour (default 6). (↓ = protects the compressor more)

cat_peak.title: Electrical-peak staging
cat_peak.description: Avoids tripping the main breaker: how many loads/watts at once + staggering. The comfort bypass lets a severe deviation skip the limit. Only applies to electrical/individual loads.

data_description.peak_max_zones: How many electric loads (heating zones or covers) may be running/moving at once before new starts are deferred. Used when no power budget is set.
data_description.peak_max_power_w: If above 0, limit by total watts instead of a count (uses the real power meter when present, otherwise the estimate). 0 = count mode.
data_description.peak_stagger_s: Minimum gap between fresh starts, so a burst of demands ramps up one at a time instead of together. (↑ = spaces starts more)
data_description.peak_comfort_bypass_c: A zone this far past its setpoint bypasses the peak limit entirely — comfort wins over peak-shaving (safety still wins above). 0 disables it. (↓ = bypasses with less deviation)

cat_energy.title: Energy estimate
cat_energy.description: Estimated watts when there is no real meter, for the energy/power sensor.

data_description.est_w_v1: Estimated VMC power at speed V1, used when no power meter is configured. (set to the real draw)
data_description.est_w_v2: Estimated VMC power at speed V2. (set to the real draw)
data_description.est_w_v3: Estimated VMC power at speed V3. (set to the real draw)
data_description.est_w_on: Estimated climate power while the zone is calling for heat/cool (no meter configured). (set to the real draw)
data_description.est_w_motor: Estimated shutter-motor power, used to estimate the energy of each movement. (set to the real draw)
data_description.full_travel_s: Time for a full open↔close travel of the shutter (scales the per-move energy). (measure the real travel)

schedule.title: Weekly schedule
schedule.description: Pick a day to edit its time-slots. Each entry has its own program: a VMC slot sets the base speed, a climate slot sets the absolute base setpoint (biases still apply on top). Enable it with the "Schedule" switch.

schedule_day.title: Schedule · {day}
schedule_day.description: Up to 4 slots. Each slot's value applies from its start time until the next slot (wrapping past midnight). Leave a row empty to drop it.

install.title: Installation type · generator
install.description: Declare your heating/cooling install. Step 1 of 3: the heat generator. Choosing the type pre-loads coherent defaults (lead time, anti-cycle) you can fine-tune afterwards.

install_dist.title: Installation type · distribution
install_dist.description: Step 2 of 3: is the generator individual or central/communal (shared)? A shared central system only lets you open a valve, so per-zone compressor and electrical-peak management do not apply.

install_emission.title: Installation type · emitter
install_emission.description: Step 3 of 3: the emitter, which sets the thermal inertia (underfloor is slow, fan-coil is fast). This pre-loads matching lead and anti-cycle defaults.

emitters.title: Emitters
emitters.description: Declare one or more emitters for this zone (e.g. underfloor radiant as the heating primary and an AC split as the cooling primary / heating support). With no emitters declared the zone keeps driving its single configured thermostat.

emitter_add.title: Add emitter
emitter_add.description: An emitter drives a real climate entity and/or a switch/valve. Mark which mode(s) it is the primary for; supports kick in when the primary lags.

data.name: Name
data.owner: This zone drives the shared unit

data.name: Name
data.owner: This zone drives the shared unit

cat_staging.title: Emitter staging
cat_staging.description: When a support emitter kicks in if the primary lags (multi-emitter zones).

data_description.support_dev_on: How far the room may fall behind the setpoint (in the active direction) before a support emitter is armed.
data_description.support_confirm_min: How long that lag must persist before the support actually turns on.
data_description.support_dev_off: Once the room recovers to within this band of the setpoint, the support begins to retire. (↓ = support retires sooner)
data_description.support_release_min: How long the recovery must hold before the support turns off (hysteresis).

cat_shared.title: Shared emitter
cat_shared.description: Reconciliation on a shared duct: this zone's weight and the anti-undershoot margin.

data_description.zone_demand_weight: Relative weight of this zone when a shared un-zoned duct aggregates the demand of its zones (higher = biased toward this zone).
data_description.shared_undershoot_margin: For a shared duct without motorized grilles, the unit cuts when the most-satisfied zone reaches its setpoint ∓ this margin, to avoid over-conditioning small rooms.

cat_tariff_bias.title: Tariff bias
cat_tariff_bias.description: How the tariff modulates anticipation: widen the lead off-peak, trim it at peak; a base bias loads thermal mass when cheap. Works with Electrical peak.

data_description.tariff_lead_cheap_mult: When the Energy module reports a «cheap» tariff, multiply the anticipation lead by this (>1 pre-conditions earlier while energy is cheap).
data_description.tariff_lead_peak_mult: When the tariff is «peak», multiply the lead by this (<1 trims anticipation to avoid expensive ramps).
data_description.tariff_bias_c: Optional base setpoint nudge (°C): when cheap, load thermal mass (heat warmer / cool cooler); when peak, coast the other way. 0 disables it.

data_description.eco: Max VMC speed in Eco mode.
data_description.sleep: Max VMC speed in Sleep mode (noise / WAF).
data_description.away: Max VMC speed in Away mode.

bathrooms.title: Bathrooms (shower boost)
bathrooms.description: Add up to 6 bathrooms (name + humidity sensor). The shower boost watches the largest humidity rise across them, so a shower in any bathroom triggers it. Fill only the rows you have.

hardware.title: Edit entities / hardware
hardware.description: Change, add or remove the chosen sensors/relays for this module. Saving reloads it so new entities take effect — without deleting and re-adding.

data.name: Name
data.hum_in: Indoor humidity (optional, dew point / dry mode)
data.facade_span_deg: Facade acceptance angle (° span, e.g. 90 = narrow, 180 = wide)
data.dc_climate: Thermostat to drive (optional; advisory if empty)
data.ds_target: Shutter target on the bus (e.g. ds)

abort.no_options: No tunable options for this module.

reset_learning.name: Reset adaptive learning
reset_learning.description: Wipes the learned adaptive-lead model of a climate (DC) zone so it relearns from scratch.

set_observe.name: Set observe (dry-run)
set_observe.description: Enables or disables observe mode: the pipeline is still evaluated but no hardware is driven.

enabled.name: Enabled
enabled.description: True to enter observe (dry-run), False to resume acting on hardware.

reset_filter.name: Reset filter hours
reset_filter.description: Zeroes the filter-hours counter of a VMC (DV) module.

recalibrate.name: Recalibrate now
recalibrate.description: Forces an immediate re-evaluation of the targeted modules' pipeline.

boost.name: Boost (timed V3)
boost.description: Forces a VMC to V3 for a number of minutes, then auto-reverts. Bypasses the quiet-hours cap.

minutes.name: Minutes
minutes.description: How long to hold V3 before reverting (default 15).

export_options.name: Export options
export_options.description: Returns the tuned option values of the targeted module(s), to save or feed into Import options.

import_options.name: Import options
import_options.description: Merges a dict of option values into the targeted module(s). Only keys valid for that module are applied; changes apply live.

values.name: Values
values.description: Option key/value pairs to merge (e.g. from Export options).

required_source_missing.title: {name}: required source unavailable
required_source_missing.description: The Dynamic Home module "{name}" has a required source unavailable: {missing}. The module falls back to a safe state until it recovers. Check the sensor wiring or its entity in Home Assistant (Settings → Devices & Services → Configure).

freecool_no_changeover.title: {name}: free-cooling may vent heat (no changeover)
freecool_no_changeover.description: The VMC "{name}" has free-cooling enabled and a heating zone is active, but no community changeover is configured. Free-cooling is purely temperature-driven without it, so on a mild winter day it can pull in cooler outside air and vent the heat you are paying for. Configure the changeover (Zones entry → supply-water sensor) so Dynamic Home knows the season, or disable free-cooling if you have central heating.

mold_risk.title: {name}: sustained mold risk
mold_risk.description: The zone "{name}" has had a high relative humidity for a sustained period (mold-risk index {index} h). Ventilation drying is requested when the outdoor air is drier, and a dehumidifier (if configured) is switched on. Consider airing the room or checking for moisture sources.

filter_due.title: {name}: filter due for replacement
filter_due.description: The VMC "{name}" has reached its filter-life threshold ({pct}% remaining of {life} h). Replace or clean the filter, then press its "Reset filter hours" button (or call the dynamic_home.reset_filter service) to clear this notice.
